2606.10749.title: Toward Secure LLM Agents: Threat Surfaces, Attacks, Defenses, and Evaluation
2606.10749.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM) agents are rapidly moving from conversational interfaces to software components that plan, invoke tools, maintain memory, and act on external environments. This transition changes the nature of security risk. In agentic settings, failures are no longer limited to unsafe text generation. Untrusted content may redirect control flow, misuse tool privileges, corrupt persistent state, leak sensitive information, or trigger harmful external actions. At the same time, research on LLM agent security is expanding quickly but remains fragmented across attack families, defense layers, application domains, and evaluation settings. This paper synthesizes 247 papers through a lifecycle-based, systems-oriented framework that models agent security around the interaction of information flow, delegated authority, and persistent state. We organize the literature around four questions: how LLM agent security should be modeled, which threat surfaces and attack families dominate, what defenses have been proposed and with what tradeoffs, and how security claims are evaluated. We find that prompt injection and tool-mediated control-flow hijacking still dominate the field, while persistent state corruption and multi-agent propagation are becoming central emerging concerns. We further find that current defenses provide useful building blocks but remain weakly compositional, and that existing benchmarks still underrepresent long-horizon, stateful, and deployment-sensitive risks. We argue that secure LLM agents require explicit trust boundaries, principled privilege control, provenance-aware state management, and evaluation practices aligned with realistic operational settings.
2606.10749.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理正在迅速從對話介面轉向計劃、調用工具、維護記憶和對外部環境採取行動的軟體組件。這一過渡改變了安全風險的性質。在代理環境中，失敗不再僅限於不安全的文本生成。不受信任的內容可能會重定向控制流程、濫用工具特權、破壞持久狀態、洩露敏感信息或觸發有害的外部行動。與此同時，對LLM代理安全的研究正在迅速擴展，但在攻擊類別、防禦層次、應用領域和評估設置上仍然是零散的。本文通過一個基於生命週期的系統導向框架綜合了247篇論文，該框架圍繞信息流、委託權限和持久狀態的互動來建模代理安全。我們圍繞四個問題組織文獻：LLM代理安全應如何建模、哪些威脅表面和攻擊類別主導、提出了哪些防禦及其權衡，以及如何評估安全聲明。我們發現，提示注入和工具介導的控制流程劫持仍然主導該領域，而持久狀態腐敗和多代理傳播正成為中心的新興關注點。我們進一步發現，當前的防禦提供了有用的構建模塊，但仍然組合性較弱，且現有基準仍然低估了長期、狀態性和部署敏感的風險。我們認為，安全的LLM代理需要明確的信任邊界、原則性的特權控制、考慮來源的狀態管理，以及與現實操作環境相一致的評估實踐。
2606.10749.author: Yuchen Ling et.al.
2606.10749.authors: Yuchen Ling, Shengcheng Yu, Zhenyu Chen, Chunrong Fang
